'''John Kirkpatrick''' (born 3 January 1979) is an English former [[rugby union]] and professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played club level rugby union (RU) for [[Preston Grasshoppers R.F.C.]], and club level rugby league (RL) in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] and in [[Championship (rugby league)|National League One]] for the [[Widnes Vikings]], as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}.

==Background==
John Kirkpatrick was born in [[Preston, Lancashire|Preston]], [[Lancashire]], England, he has [[Scottish people|Scottish]] [[ancestor]]s, and eligible to play for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] due to the [[Grandfather rule|grandparent rule]].{{fact|date=February 2020}}

==Playing career==
Kirkpatrick joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] from [[rugby union]] club [[Preston Grasshoppers]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |date=27 February 2002 |title=Meet the squad |url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/sport/other-sport/meet-the-squad-3559749 |access-date=7 August 2023 |website=Liverpool Echo}}</ref> He initially played for the club on a part-time basis while studying for a university degree.<ref>{{cite web |title=Kirkpatrick graduates with honours |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/kirkpatrick-graduates-with-honours-175391.html |website=The Independent |access-date=7 August 2023 |date=1 September 2002}}</ref>

Kirkpatrick was called into the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] (RL) squad in 2006.<ref name="McCormack Names Squad For World Cup Clash">{{cite web|url=http://www.sacenturions.co.uk/xArchive_2006.html|title=McCormack Names Squad For World Cup Clash|publisher=SA Centurions|date=2006-10-05|accessdate=2008-05-05|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20071029231335/http://www.sacenturions.co.uk/xArchive_2006.html|archivedate=29 October 2007 }}</ref>
